index.name: Nifty Bank
index.description: Nifty Bank tracks 12 largest, most liquid banking stocks on NSE. De facto barometer of RBI monetary policy transmission — bank NIM (Net Interest Margin) expands during high-rate environments, compresses during rate cuts. Most actively traded index globally (weekly options volume exceeds $100B notional). Private banks dominate: HDFC Bank (28.5%), ICICI Bank (22.3%), Kotak (12.6%) control ~63% weight. Banks leverage economic growth through credit creation — rural credit (MSME, agriculture, auto loans) provides 40% incremental NPL risk during downturns but generates explosive growth during recoveries. Credit-to-GDP ratio of 50% in India vs 70% in US suggests 4-5 years of runway for incremental lending. Asset quality improved dramatically post-2019 (NPA cleanup, ₹3.5 Lakh Cr Govt recapitalization, SARFAESI recovery machinery). Current tailwinds: PM Mudra (50M small businesses), PM Kisan (₹500K farming loans), PM Awas (affordable housing mortgages). Headwind: deposit growth lagging credit growth (creating negative liquidity), forcing banks to pay depositors higher interest rates squeezing NIMs.

index.name: Nifty Private Bank Index
index.description: Tracks only private sector banks within the Nifty Banking universe. Excludes PSU banks (SBI, PNB, BOB). Private banks historically deliver higher ROE, better asset quality, and superior earnings growth due to technology investment and retail banking penetration.
